steps.title: Identify the infinitely-many-solutions condition
steps.content: An equation has infinitely many solutions when both sides are identical for all values of the variable — meaning the coefficients of corresponding terms must match. The original equation (referenced as 'the equation above') gives constraints that fix $a$ and $b$ to specific values.

steps.title: Identify the matching values
steps.content: The coefficient and constant matching produce $a = 2$ and $b = 7$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
